docs.title: Exceptions
docs.text:
All Fasiri exceptions can be imported from the top-level fasiri package:

from fasiri import (
    FasiriError,
    AuthenticationError,
    RateLimitError,
    UnsupportedLanguageError,
    ProviderError,
)

docs.title: FasiriError
docs.text:
Base class for all Fasiri exceptions.

Attributes:

Attribute Type Description message str Human-readable error description. code str Machine-readable error code, e.g. "INVALID_API_KEY". status_code int HTTP status code from the API.

docs.title: AuthenticationError
docs.text:
Raised when the API key is missing, invalid, or expired.

HTTP status: 401

Error codes: - MISSING_API_KEY - No Authorization header provided - INVALID_API_KEY - Key does not exist or has expired

from fasiri import Fasiri, AuthenticationError

try:
    client = Fasiri(api_key="fsri_invalid")
    result = client.translate("Hello", target="lug")
except AuthenticationError as e:
    print(e.code)    # "INVALID_API_KEY"
    print(e.status_code)  # 401

docs.title: RateLimitError
docs.text:
Raised when the rate limit is exceeded.

HTTP status: 429

Additional attribute: retry_after (int) - seconds to wait before retrying.

from fasiri import Fasiri, RateLimitError
import time

try:
    result = client.translate("Hello", target="lug")
except RateLimitError as e:
    print(f"Rate limited. Wait {e.retry_after} seconds.")
    time.sleep(e.retry_after)

docs.title: UnsupportedLanguageError
docs.text:
Raised when a language code is not recognised or a language pair is not supported.

HTTP status: 422

from fasiri import Fasiri, UnsupportedLanguageError

try:
    result = client.translate("Hello", target="xyz")
except UnsupportedLanguageError as e:
    print(f"Language not supported: {e}")

docs.title: ProviderError
docs.text:
Raised when all providers fail to translate the requested text. This is typically a transient error caused by provider outages or network issues.

HTTP status: 503

from fasiri import Fasiri, ProviderError

try:
    result = client.translate("Hello", target="lug")
except ProviderError as e:
    # Show original text or cached result to user
    print(f"Translation temporarily unavailable: {e}")

docs.title: transcribe() - Speech to Text
docs.text:
# From file path
stt = client.transcribe("recording.wav", language="lug")
print(stt.transcript)
print(stt.latency_ms)

# From bytes
with open("audio.mp3", "rb") as f:
    stt = client.transcribe(f.read(), language="sw")

Returns an STTResult.

Supported languages: lug, ach, nyn, teo, lgg, sw, en, rw

docs.title: synthesise() / synthesize() - Text to Speech
docs.text:
tts = client.synthesise("Oli otya?", language="lug")

# Audio URL (expires ~2 minutes)
print(tts.audio_url)

# Download immediately
import httpx
audio = httpx.get(tts.audio_url).content
with open("output.mp3", "wb") as f:
    f.write(audio)

Returns a TTSResult.

Supported languages: lug, ach, nyn, teo, lgg, sw

docs.title: translate()
docs.text:
result = client.translate(
    text="Hello, how are you?",
    target="lug",
    source="en",      # optional - auto-detected if omitted
    provider="auto",  # "auto" | "sunbird" | "huggingface"
)

print(result)                    # "Oli otya?"
print(result.provider)           # "sunbird"
print(result.quality_score)      # 0.92
print(result.latency_ms)         # 820
print(result.model_used)         # "sunbird/nllb_translate"

Returns a TranslationResult.

docs.text:
result = await client.async_translate("Hello", target="lug")

See Async Usage for patterns.

docs.title: Data Types
docs.text: All objects returned by the Fasiri SDK.

docs.title: TranslationResult
docs.text:
Returned by client.translate().

@dataclass
class TranslationResult:
    translated_text:      str
    detected_source_lang: str
    target_lang:          str
    model_used:           str
    provider:             str
    quality_score:        float
    latency_ms:           int
    characters_translated: int

docs.title: Fields
docs.text: Field Type Description translated_text str The translated text detected_source_lang str Source language (detected or provided) target_lang str Target language code model_used str Exact model that performed translation provider str Provider used: "sunbird" or "huggingface" quality_score float Estimated translation quality (0.0-1.0) latency_ms int Time taken in milliseconds characters_translated int Source character count

docs.title: String representation
docs.text:
str(result) returns translated_text:

result = client.translate("Hello", target="lug")
print(result)   # "Oli otya?"
print(f"Translation: {result}")  # "Translation: Oli otya?"

docs.title: BatchResult
docs.text:
Returned by client.translate_batch().

@dataclass
class BatchResult:
    results:          list[BatchItemResult]
    total:            int
    succeeded:        int
    failed:           int
    total_latency_ms: int

docs.title: Fields
docs.text: Field Type Description results list[BatchItemResult] All results in input order total int Total number of items succeeded int Number of successful translations failed int Number of failed translations total_latency_ms int Total wall time in milliseconds

docs.title: Methods
docs.text:
Method Returns Description successful() list[BatchItemResult] Only successful results errors() list[BatchItemResult] Only failed results __iter__() iterator Iterate over all results __len__() int Total number of results 
batch = client.translate_batch([...])

# Iterate all
for item in batch:
    print(item.id, item)

# Successful only
for item in batch.successful():
    print(item.translated_text)

# Failed only
for item in batch.errors():
    print(f"[{item.id}] failed: {item.error}")

docs.title: BatchItemResult
docs.text:
Individual item within a BatchResult.

@dataclass
class BatchItemResult:
    id:                   str
    translated_text:      str | None
    detected_source_lang: str | None
    target_lang:          str
    model_used:           str | None
    provider:             str | None
    quality_score:        float | None
    error:                str | None

docs.title: Properties
docs.text: Property Returns Description success bool True if error is None

docs.title: String representation
docs.text:
str(item) returns translated_text on success, or "[ERROR] <message>" on failure.

item = batch.results[0]

if item.success:
    print(item.translated_text)
else:
    print(f"Failed: {item.error}")

# String shorthand
print(str(item))

docs.title: STTResult
docs.text:
Returned by client.transcribe().

@dataclass
class STTResult:
    transcript:   str
    detected_lang: str | None
    language:     str
    model_used:   str
    provider:     str
    latency_ms:   int

docs.title: String representation
docs.text: str(stt) returns transcript.

docs.title: TTSResult
docs.text:
Returned by client.synthesise().

@dataclass
class TTSResult:
    audio_url:    str | None
    audio_base64: str | None
    content_type: str
    language:     str
    model_used:   str
    provider:     str
    latency_ms:   int

Audio URL expires

audio_url is a signed URL that expires in ~2 minutes. Download immediately:

import httpx

tts = client.synthesise("Oli otya?", language="lug")
audio_bytes = httpx.get(tts.audio_url).content
with open("output.mp3", "wb") as f:
    f.write(audio_bytes)

docs.title: Language
docs.text:
Returned in the list from client.languages().

@dataclass
class Language:
    code:                 str
    name:                 str
    native_name:          str
    region:               str
    family:               str
    supports_translation: bool
    supports_stt:         bool
    supports_tts:         bool
    tts_voice_id:         int | None
    best_provider:        str
    quality_score:        float

docs.title: repr
docs.text:
lang = next(l for l in client.languages() if l.code == "lug")
print(repr(lang))
# <Language lug: Luganda [translate, stt, tts]>

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/umarkhemis/fasiri
cd fasiri
cp .env.example .env
# Fill in your API keys in .env

docker-compose up --build

The API will be available at https://fasiri-bu9u.onrender.com.

docs.title: Production
docs.text:
# Build
docker build -t fasiri:latest .

# Run with 4 workers
docker run -d \
  --name fasiri \
  -p 8000:8000 \
  --env-file .env \
  fasiri:latest

docs.title: docker-compose.yml
docs.text: The included docker-compose.yml starts: - fasiri - the API server (port 8000) - nginx - reverse proxy (port 80/443) - redis - rate limiting (optional)

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text:
Copy .env.example to .env:

cp .env.example .env

docs.title: Provider Credentials
docs.text: Variable Required Description SUNBIRD_API_KEY Yes JWT from POST https://api.sunbird.ai/auth/token KHAYA_API_KEY Yes Subscription key from translation.ghananlp.org KHAYA_API_KEY_SECONDARY - Secondary Khaya key for rate-limit failover HUGGINGFACE_API_KEY Yes Free token from huggingface.co/settings/tokens

docs.title: Security
docs.text: Variable Default Description FASIRI_SECRET_KEY change-me-... Generate with: openssl rand -hex 32 API_KEY_TTL_SECONDS 31536000 Key lifetime in seconds (default: 1 year)

docs.title: Rate Limiting
docs.text: Variable Default Description RATE_LIMIT_RPM 60 Requests per minute per API key RATE_LIMIT_BATCH_RPM 10 Batch requests per minute per API key REDIS_URL - Redis for multi-worker rate limiting

docs.title: Server
docs.text: Variable Default Description BASE_URL https://fasiri-bu9u.onrender.com Public base URL DEBUG false Enable debug logging HTTP_TIMEOUT 30 Provider timeout (secs) HTTP_TIMEOUT_STT 60 STT timeout (secs)
